name: nervous system
definition: organized group of cells specialized for the conduction of electrochemical stimuli from sensory receptors through a network to the site at which a response occurs

name: endocrine system
definition: complex group of ; comprises a group of ductless glands that regulate body processes by secreting chemical substances called hormones
diseases: inability of target organs or tissues to respond to hormones effectively
regulated-by: a hormone that influences the function of the gland (a tropic hormone)
a biologically important element (e.g., calcium or potassium)
functions: regulate the body’s internal activities and the body’s interactions with the external environment to preserve the internal environment
the control of growth and development
sexual differentiation of the fetus into a male or a female

name: immune system
functions: help eliminate abnormal cells of the body that can develop into cancer

mechanisms.name: chemical barriers
instances: lysozyme (an enzyme that can break down the outer wall of certain bacteria)

mechanisms.name: cells that attack foreign cells and body cells harboring infectious agents

types.name: specific, acquired
types.description: tailored to particular types of invaders
